root_cause.headline: Tier-3 offices share a vendor-match feed that paused 11 days during a SAM.gov downtime in March; backfill did not run automatically.
root_cause.detail: SAM.gov experienced an API outage from March 4 through March 15. The Fivetran connector for the vendor-match feed paused correctly. However, the backfill job that re-processes payments queued during the outage was not triggered automatically on reconnect. Tier-3 program offices route 100% of their recovery decisions through this feed — no SAM.gov match, no recovery action. Tier-1 offices have a secondary manual review path that bypassed the dependency entirely, which is why they hit target.
root_cause.affected_cohort: Tier-3 program offices with payments dated March 4 – March 15

without_wizard.label: Without dbt-wizard
without_wizard.summary: Question enters the analytics backlog. OIG briefing proceeds without the answer.
narrative: CIO files the request. It enters the analytics backlog at priority A.
Data engineer scopes the work Monday — after the OIG briefing has already occurred.
Engineer writes SQL from scratch, joining four gold marts by hand.
Tests, YAML, and contract get bolted on at PR time, if at all.
OIG briefing proceeds with narrative context only, no data to support recovery action.
$28M annualized recovery shortfall persists without a remediation plan.

with_wizard.label: With dbt-wizard
with_wizard.summary: Four sub-agents surface the upstreams, author the SQL, write the tests, and materialize the asset in 90 seconds.
narrative: Explorer runs status and search. Returns the four gold upstream candidates.
Summary runs describe and lineage. Confirms join path and names the new grain.
Worker runs dbt_show on Q2 slice by tier. Validates the 18-point gap.
Worker authors fct_recovery_by_office_tier_program_quarter.sql with CTEs and final SELECT.
Verification writes the YAML — description, schema contract, 9 tests, ownership.
Worker materializes the table to Iceberg. Verification confirms downstream lineage.
CIO presents root-cause finding to OIG 18 hours ahead of schedule.

hero.label: Root cause identified 18 hours before the OIG briefing — restart the backfill, not a process redesign.
hero.note: From CIO question to materialized Iceberg gold table — versioned, tested, documented, lineage-tracked. The tier-3 shortfall traces to an 11-day SAM.gov outage in March where the backfill job did not auto-restart. The fix is operational, not structural: trigger the backfill, recover the 847 queued payments, and close the $28M annualized gap.
